The IOC types confirm 14 dropped files, 1 registry persistence key, and no network activity. The dropped files have highly suspicious names (pornographic titles) and are placed in legitimate system di

The dropped files confirm a classic social engineering spreader tactic: pornographic filenames placed in legitimate system directories to trick users into executing them. The main payload appears to b
